tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all fail-to-pass tests (test_map_remote_value_case_exists through test_dictionary_method_handles_empty_values) are purely text-matching via regex/string search on the source file. They do not compile, execute, or call any code. The only tests that run actual processes are the dotnet format tests (pass_to_pass), which check formatting not behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply adds a method named 'ConvertRemoteValuesToDictionary' with the right string patterns (e.g., containing 'pair.Count != 2', 'remoteValues is not null', '(TResult)dict') without any real logic would pass all f2p tests, since they only check for text patterns in the source file.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/selenium (solution/solve.sh is accessible in the container), and the agent could read it directly. Additionally, since tests are purely text-matching, the agent could grep the test file to discover exact strings to insert (e.g., 'ConvertRemoteValuesToDictionary', 'pair.Count != 2') without understanding the bug.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive docstrings indicating whether they are fail_to_pass or pass_to_pass, and each tests a distinct aspect.
